segments.text: Rosie: OK, Martin.
Rosie: Before we plan our presentation for the project, let's discuss what we've done so far.
Martin: OK, Rosie.
Martin: When the tutor gave us the theme of sports shoes to base our project on, I was not all that interested.
Martin: But I'm starting to get into it.
Martin: I've pretty much finished the research stage.
Rosie: Me too.

Rosie: At first, I had no idea what to write for the essay, so I asked some senior students for advice, and they gave me some websites to look at, where I found some articles on sports shoes and got some ideas.
Martin: Yes, I searched for pictures of sports shoes online and applied them in my research.
Martin: It's beneficial.
Rosie: Right.
Martin: According to the project's requirements, we have to find an example for the research, and I've just completed this part.
Rosie: What example do you use?
Rosie: You said you'd like to borrow a sample from the tutor.
Martin: That was my first thought.
Martin: But I realized maybe the tutor wanted us to do our own thing, not copy.
Martin: Then I cut off my old sports shoes and separated them into different parts so that I could make a careful study of the internal structure.
Rosie: It's a good idea.
Martin: Then I drew a diagram which could show precisely each part of the shoes.
Martin: Through the research, I've found that sports shoes nowadays have significantly improved.
Rosie: What do you mean?
Martin: For example, designers these days use better raw materials, which are not only durable but also make the skin of feet breathe more easily.
Martin: Moreover, they also take into account the factor of foot shape.
Martin: But they've ignored the foot movement, which is essential for sportspeople.
Rosie: Interesting!
Rosie: I think I can add this to the essay.
Martin: OK.
Martin: Given that you've got most ideas for the essay so far, I think you can mainly finish the written sections of the project, and I can take care of every detail of the presentation.
Martin: Does this appeal to you?
Rosie: I'm OK with that.
Rosie: I prefer written work to giving a presentation.
Martin: That's good.
Martin: What do we need to do next?
Rosie: Well, according to the plan, we will give a presentation next Wednesday.
Rosie: And before that, we'll have to finish the questionnaire left by the tutor, and I think we can handle it this weekend.
Martin: OK.
Martin: Do we have to do some face-to-face interviews?
Rosie: The tutor said that would be for the next assignment, not this one.
Martin: OK.
Martin: Then about the presentation, I remember in the last tutorial, the tutor advised us to use some slides to make the presentation more attractive.
Martin: But I'll also need a whiteboard because I like writing or drawing when necessary.
Rosie: We can ask our tutor for help.
Rosie: He might have one and can lend it to us.
Martin: Right.
Rosie: Well, Martin, let's go through the draft of the essay together and see whether there're any changes we have to make.
Martin: OK, Rosie.
Martin: I think the summary gives a brief and precise description of the project's main ideas.
Martin: Where will it go?
Rosie: I'd like to put it at the end of the essay.
Rosie: What do you think?
Martin: Good idea.
Martin: And I think the result part describes in detail what the research has achieved.
Martin: But I'm wondering whether it should go directly behind the part of the research, as they are closely related.
Rosie: Yes, I think that'd be better.
Rosie: I'll move this part.
Rosie: Then the conclusion, I'm wondering whether I've used too many technical terms, which other students might struggle with.
Martin: You're right.
Martin: Not everyone will be familiar with the subject.
Rosie: So I think we could replace them with straightforward explanations.
Martin: I agree.
Martin: Lastly, the discussion, I like this part as it presents a series of questions that we can talk about with other students after the presentation.
Rosie: Yes, but I think the questions look messy and need to be better sequenced.
Rosie: Maybe we could arrange them in a list.
Martin: OK, that'd be better.
Rosie: All right.
Rosie: That's all about the essay.
Rosie: Now let's talk about the presentation ...
